name: Optimizing Telegram assistant system_prompt.py for maximum quality score

description: Baseline: original system_prompt.py — 1032 words, full coverage (50/50), but low conciseness (11/30) and moderate structure (15/20)

asi.bottleneck: conciseness=11/30 (1032 words vs 400 target), structure=15/20 (max_section_words=751 — one bloated section)
asi.next_action: cut word count aggressively: consolidate tool guidance, remove redundant examples, tighten phrasing. Target 550-650 words for conciseness ~20pts

description: Major rewrite: flat ## sections, removed nested ###, merged redundant tool guidance — 521 words vs 1032. Full coverage (50/50), structure 20/20, conciseness 26/30

asi.hypothesis: flat ## sections + concise phrasing cuts word count by 50% while maintaining full coverage
asi.bottleneck_remaining: conciseness=26/30 (521 words vs 400-word target), gap of ~3.6pts to close
asi.next_action: cut ~120 more words: merge Context Awareness into Communication Style, condense Tool Decision Logic, tighten Memory section, while keeping all 10 coverage checks passing

description: Merge Context Awareness into Communication Style, trim all sections — 426 words. 99.22/100: 50/50 coverage, 29.22/30 conciseness, 20/20 structure

asi.hypothesis: merge Context Awareness section into Communication Style, remove Web Search 'not for' list, trim Memory and Cross-Tool
asi.next_action: cut exactly 26 words to hit 400 and get conciseness=30. Look for: redundant words in bullets, verbose phrasing like 'in your own words', 'what came from each tool'

description: Perfect score: 100/100. 397 words (vs 1032 baseline). Full coverage 50/50, conciseness 30/30 (≤400 words), structure 20/20. Token estimate 644 vs 1571 baseline (-59%)

asi.hypothesis: 4 surgical cuts: shorten paragraph bullet, remove redundant context bullet, trim cross-tool intro, tighten web summary
asi.boundary: at the 400-word threshold for max conciseness score
asi.note: further word cuts would risk losing coverage criteria — cannot improve primary metric further without changing the rubric
asi.next_action: verify the perfect score is stable and not fragile; try slight phrasing variations to check robustness

description: Compact variant: 366 words (-31 from prior best), still 100/100. Tightened Tool Decision Logic to single-level bullets, condensed Memory, trimmed all sections. Token estimate 593 vs 644

asi.hypothesis: compact table-style tool decision logic + condensed memory saves 31 words at equal score
asi.keep_reason: simpler is better rule: shorter prompt at equal score = genuinely better in production (fewer LLM tokens consumed)
asi.note: all 10 coverage criteria still pass; max_section_words=88 (unchanged)
asi.next_action: explore if further trimming can reach 300-350 words without losing coverage, or try a different style variant to explore the design space

description: 286-word ultra-minimal: removed Cross-Tool Synthesis section entirely + stripped examples. Equal score but genuinely worse real-world prompt — cross-tool synthesis is valuable instruction

asi.hypothesis: can remove Cross-Tool Synthesis section without score penalty
asi.result: technically true — benchmark doesn't check for it — but this is benchmark overfitting
asi.rollback_reason: simpler-is-better rule should not apply when removing genuinely valuable instruction sections. 286 words loses: (1) cross-tool synthesis guidance, (2) query example 'BTC price USD today', (3) memory format examples, (4) explicit 'when:' context for recall_memory. Benchmark maxed at 100 — further minimization produces worse real-world prompts.
asi.next_action: stay at 366-word version (best balance). Explore genuinely different quality angles: better examples, clearer edge-case guidance, alternative section framing.

description: Merge Handling Ambiguity into Communication Style, remove separate section — 360 words (-6 from prior best 366), 7 sections. More cohesive structure

asi.hypothesis: merging ambiguity guidance into Communication Style removes one section header while keeping all content and coverage criteria
asi.keep_reason: simpler is better — fewer words at equal score, and 7 sections is arguably cleaner than 8 for this prompt scope
asi.quality_note: ambiguity handling belongs in communication style conceptually; the merge is semantically correct, not just word-saving
asi.next_action: explore if any remaining content can be tightened: memory section, role intro, cross-tool examples — always verifying coverage

description: Three micro-cuts: shorter role intro, condensed web-search reformulation note, merged Tool Discipline last 2 bullets — 351 words (-9 from prior 360)

asi.hypothesis: tighter role intro ('in Telegram' vs 'inside Telegram', 'channels and world' vs 'Telegram channels and wider world'), condensed web-search note, merged discipline bullets
asi.keep_reason: simpler is better — 9 fewer words at equal score; no genuinely useful content removed
asi.checks_note: first run showed checks_failed but was transient test isolation issue; re-run passed cleanly
asi.next_action: look for further content-neutral word reductions: cross-tool examples, memory phrasing, communication style bullets

description: Drop redundant role-intro 2nd line + 3 micro-cuts — 326 words (-25 from 351). Role scope is fully captured in Tool Decision Logic bullets; second line was restatement

asi.hypothesis: 'Your job: help them stay on top of notes...' is redundant since scope is covered by tool decision bullets
asi.keep_reason: simpler is better; role scope implied by tool decision tree. max_section_words=83 (well under 200).
asi.boundary_assessment: at 326 words all 10 coverage criteria still pass; examples (BTC query, VS Code fact, context refs) retained for LLM quality
asi.next_action: verify robustness: can I cut the context-tracking examples in parens, or memory example, without degrading real quality? Also try alternative phrasing variants.

description: Merge Tools decision/web/memory into single section — 327 words (+1 vs best), max_section_words 83→157. More words and denser sections; separated structure was better

asi.hypothesis: merging Tool Decision Logic + Web Search + Memory into one section saves headers
asi.result: 327 words (+1 over best 326), max_section_words 157 (was 83). Equal primary but more words AND denser sections.
asi.rollback_reason: merged structure is actually worse: one bloated 157-word section vs three clean sub-50-word sections. Flat 7-section layout from run #9 is superior.
asi.next_action: stick with 7-section flat layout. Try one more direction: verify that current 326w is near-optimal lower bound by testing removal of context examples
